title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Dr. Arthur Calgary, a geophysicist recently returned from a two-year expedition to Antarctica, meets with Leo Ayrshire, his daughter Hester, and Gwendolyn Vaughn to discuss the death of Leo's wife, Rachel. Calgary explains that his long absence left him out of touch with recent legal matters, specifically the murder trial of Leo's son, Jack Ayrshire (also known as Jack or Jacqueline).

Calgary recounts the events of November 9th of the previous year. Jack had visited his mother to demand money, behaving rudely and threateningly before leaving. Later that evening, Rachel was found dead, having been struck by a poker that bore Jack's fingerprints. A sum of money was missing from her desk, and Jack was subsequently arrested in Dreamead with the cash in his possession. During his trial, Jack claimed he had an alibi, stating he had hitched a ride in a dark sedan at 6:55 p.m., but the police were unable to corroborate this. Despite defense arguments regarding Jack's unstable mental state, he was sentenced to life imprisonment and died of pneumonia six months later.

Leo confirms the facts of the case but expresses dissatisfaction with the legal system, believing it failed to recognize Jack's mental instability under the McNodden Act. While the family maintains that Jack was guilty of the actions, Calgary shocks them by declaring that Jack is actually innocent. Leo responds by asking if Calgary believes Jack should not have been held responsible for his actions.
